question: Which of the following UnorderedList methods has an average and worst-case time complexity of $O(1)$?

answers.feedback: Incorrect. This method is $O(n)$ because it must traverse from the head to the end to count every node.
Correct! This method only requires one step to check if the head reference is None.
Incorrect. Searching requires traversal and takes $O(n)$ time as it may process every node in the list.
Incorrect. Removing an item requires a traversal process to find the specific node, which is $O(n)$.
